Explain the difference between TCP and UDP 🌐

TCP (Transmission Control Protocol) and UDP (User Datagram Protocol) are the two main transport layer protocols.

FeatureTCPUDP
ConnectionConnection-orientedConnectionless
ReliabilityGuaranteed deliveryBest-effort
SpeedSlower (overhead)Faster (no handshake)
Use casesHTTP, FTP, SSHDNS, video streaming
 
import socket

# TCP example
tcp = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
tcp.connect(('example.com', 80))

# UDP example
udp = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_DGRAM)
udp.sendto(b'hello', ('8.8.8.8', 53))

When should I use UDP over TCP?

Choose UDP when:

  • Low latency matters more than reliability — live video, gaming, VoIP
  • Loss is tolerable — a dropped frame in video is better than delay
  • Broadcast/multicast — sending to multiple hosts simultaneously
  • Simple request-response — DNS lookups (fast, one packet each way)
